description: The New Zealand batter raised concerns about the long pause in Tariq’s action before release. Mitchell walked away twice during the over, before the umpires stepped in to allow play to continue.

title: 'Entire trip was spectacular': Trump hails Artemis II crew, eyes Mars next
description: Artemis II astronauts have successfully returned to Earth after a historic lunar flyby, setting a new distance record. President Trump lauded the crew's achievement and declared Mars as the next frontier for human space exploration. This mission tested crucial deep-space systems, paving the way for future lunar landings and ambitious journeys to the Red Planet.

title: British Airways ramps up services to India for summer
description: As part of the expansion, an additional daily service between Bengaluru and London Heathrow will operate from June 1 to October 24, 2026, increasing the frequencies to 14 weekly flights on the Boeing 777

title: Building low-cost medical devices that scale is a challenge: HSD Srinivas, Tata Trusts
Focus on innovation and entrepreneurship in farm sector through agritech meet in Rajasthan
description: The GRAM-2026 will take place in Jaipur from May 23 to 25, aiming to unite farmers, scientists, investors, and policymakers to foster dialogue and help agriculturists access global expertise and modern technologies

title: Highest-ever women voter turnout in Kerala, Assam and Puducherry
description: Women voters demonstrated remarkable enthusiasm in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry, achieving record-breaking turnout and outperforming male voters in all three regions. Kerala saw the widest gender gap, with women's participation significantly boosting overall polling. This surge highlights women's growing political engagement and their importance to electoral outcomes.

title: 'Intended to marry minor': Man, 9 others held for murder of pregnant wife, 2 daughters
description: A man has been arrested for allegedly drowning his pregnant wife and two daughters in a swimming pool after discovering the unborn child was female. He reportedly intended to marry a minor to father a male heir. Police also arrested ten others for facilitating an illegal prenatal sex determination test.

title: Markets gain 6% over the week, snap six week losing streak
Equity mutual fund inflows hit 8-month high in March
Yashwant Varma's resignation may open graft-probe window
description: Justice Yashwant Varma resigned, avoiding a parliamentary removal debate. His resignation follows a probe into a large cash discovery at his official residence. An inquiry committee corroborated the findings and the subsequent mysterious disappearance of the money. This incident intensified discussions on judicial integrity and judge appointments.

title: Srinivasan & Vijay Singh renew IPO call: Shapoorji group also pushes for Tata Sons’ listing
From salt bans to school gardens, how small cities worldwide are taking on childhood obesity

description: "We're going to open up the gulf with or without them...or the strait as they call it. I think it's going to go pretty quickly, and if it doesn't, we'll be able to finish it off," U.S. President Donald Trump told reporters

description: “We are in advanced stages for formulating a scheme to develop processing value chains for critical minerals,” Piyush Goyal, Secretary of Ministry of Mines stated

Artemis II splashdown: Orion comes back, begins a new era in human space travel
Nasa’s Artemis II crew returns to Earth after record Moon mission
description: Artemis II astronauts have successfully returned to Earth, splashing down near San Diego after a groundbreaking lunar flyby. This 10-day mission tested deep space systems and set a new record for human distance from our planet. The crew's return at incredible speeds marks a significant milestone, paving the way for future lunar landings.

title: Women’s reservation law amendment: Congress may call Opp meet on April 15
‘Dhurandhar 2’ day 23 box office: Film earns Rs 6.7 crore Friday
description: Ranveer Singh's spy drama 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' achieved massive box office success, becoming one of his highest earners. Despite a recent dip in daily collections after its 21st day, the film's overall domestic net collection has surpassed Rs 1,055 crore, with worldwide figures nearing Rs 1700 crore.

description: U.S. Ambassador to India, Sergei Gor said U.S. was ready to cooperate with India on civil nuclear cooperation in addition to other areas, such as coal gasification and U.S. LPG exports

Char Dham Yatra 2026: Uttarakhand High Court issues 3-week ultimatum over pilgrims, animal safety
PCB, PSL under fire after ex-Pak captain Misbah's role comes to light
description: Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) faces criticism after Misbah-ul-Haq worked with Peshawar Zalmi despite holding a board role, while Sarfaraz Ahmed and Wahab Riaz were barred earlier. Misbah defended himself, citing a prior contract, and said, “I had signed a contract with Zalmi before I became national selector…”. The issue has raised concerns over inconsistent application of PCB’s conflict-of-interest rules.

title: Induction-based cooking to add 13-27 GW of energy requirements: Official
description: Officials from the Power Ministry informed that in order to fill for the gap left out by gas-based power plants, thermal power plants have deferred their scheduled maintenance by three months

title: Absorbed uptick in price of ammonium nitrate, diesel to shield prices: Coal India
description: Coal India held average prices of explosives increased 26% between February and March-end.

10 Indian nationals indicted in US for visa fraud conspiracy

description: The ​blockade of the ‌strait by Tehran since the start of the Iran war has caused the worst disruption to global energy supplies in history

title: Sensex jumps 919pts as West Asia peace plan moves ahead
description: Global markets rallied, including India's Sensex, as a West Asia peace plan offered hope for an end to the conflict. Foreign funds made significant inflows after weeks of selling, boosting investor wealth. However, tech stocks faced selling pressure due to renewed AI concerns.

title: Parties woo women voters with cash transfer promises
description: Political parties in Assam are vying for women's votes with competing cash-transfer promises, including increased monthly payouts and empowerment initiatives. This comes as women voters demonstrate consistently high participation, often surpassing male turnout in recent elections across Assam, Puducherry, and Kerala, influencing electoral dynamics.

title: Can't have different DA hikes for staff and pensioners: Supreme Court
description: The Supreme Court has ruled that differential dearness benefit increases for serving employees and pensioners are arbitrary. Inflation impacts both equally, making a 14% DA hike for serving staff and only 11% DR for retirees discriminatory. The court stated that while financial constraints can justify implementation delays, unequal benefit rates violate the right to equality.

title: Piyush Goyal assures supply chain support to Gulf counterparts
description: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal discussed supply chain issues, particularly food, with UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain officials. He emphasized regional peace amid tensions and thanked Gulf nations for supporting the large Indian diaspora. Discussions also covered UAE's logistics solutions to counter disruptions caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade, vital for India's global trade.

title: Political slugfest between Congress-BJP in Haryana over crop procurement
description: Farmers, who sustain the nation through their hard work, are being treated like criminals in the grain markets. It is as if they have arrived carrying illicit goods rather than their harvested crops,” Bhupinder Singh Hooda said

title: Rubio to visit in May, India may host Quad meet
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit India next month, following a productive meeting with Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri. The discussions focused on bilateral ties, including trade, critical minerals, defense, and the Quad. Efforts are also under way to schedule a Quad foreign ministers' meeting around Rubio's visit.

title: Share burden of war cost: Govt to India Inc
description: A senior government official urged industry representatives to absorb some of the rising raw material costs instead of passing them entirely to consumers. This statement comes as ministries address supply chain disruptions, particularly concerning packaging materials for essential goods like food and drugs, due to the West Asia conflict.

title: In Assam, first evicted, now erased
description: After a revision of electoral rolls in Assam, many people, mostly Bengali-speaking Muslims evicted from government land, lost their voting rights and now hold voter IDs deemed invalid. The fear of being made non-citizens is strongest at Kachutali village, about 30 km east of Guwahati, where more than 2,000 voters could not be a part of the democratic process of electing a new government, reports Rahul Karmakar

title: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule: A light that still shows India the way
description: Today marks the beginning of Mahatma Jyotirao Phule's 200th birth anniversary celebrations. A pioneer of social reform, Phule championed education for all, especially girls, and founded the Satyashodhak Samaj to fight for equal rights and social justice. His unwavering commitment to the marginalized continues to inspire India's progress.

title: BSF jawan killed in unprovoked firing in Manipur’s Ukhrul
description: Constable Mithun Mandal who hailed from West Bengal, succumbed to his bullet injury at RIMS Hospital, Imphal

title: A high-profile contest brewsin Chennai’s Mylapore
description: Constituency reflects broader urban churn with residents pointing out civic woes even as national parties look for gains in Tamil Nadu

title: Welfare measures for the marginalised take centre stage in Bengal’s Jhargram
description: SCs/STs make up half the population of Jhargram, giving them a decisive say in the poll verdict; TMC, BJP clash over development plans, UCC implementation in a district emerging from the long shadow of Maoist extremism

title: EC rejects TMC pick's nomination for subsisting contract with govt
description: The Election Commission has cancelled Trinamool Congress candidate Abhinav Bhattacharya's nomination for Krishnanagar North due to a conflict of interest. Bhattacharya's business dealings with the state government violated Section 9A of the Representation of the People Act, 1951. An alternative candidate, Somnath Dutta, will now contest the seat for TMC.

title: Tamil Nadu voter deleted from electoral roll reminds Supreme Court of promise to protect in case of ‘mass exclusion’
description: The petitioner, C. Geetha, said her name had been a mainstay in the State’s electoral roll over the years; she was even intending to contest as an independent candidate in the 51-Uthangarai (SC) Assembly Constituency and 52-Bargur Assembly Constituency in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections in 2026.

title: Arun shifted, Modak appointed Chennai Police Commissioner
Discontinue Ladki Bahin if government doesn’t have funds for pension: Bombay HC
description: Ministers, including Shiv Sena’s Sanjay Shirsat, have complained of budget diversions to fund the Mahayuti government’s flagship scheme

title: Legally married, currently separated, says actor-BJP pick on marital status
description: BJP MLA Hiraan Chattopadhyay's nomination affidavit reveals he is 'legally married; currently separated' from Anindita, who previously accused him of cruelty and a second marriage.  Contesting from Shyampur, Hiraan declared significant assets, including Rs 15 lakh cash, while his wife has Rs 12 lakh.  Their combined wealth stands at Rs 14.5 crore, with Hiraan facing four pending criminal cases.

title: Draft master plan for Great Nicobar Island projects tourism as primary growth driver
description: Notified for suggestions, plan aims for tourism-led development, targets 3.36 lakh population and one million visitors by 2055.

title: Kargil hero Sonam Wangchuk dead; Rajnath hails ‘son of Ladakh’
description: Kargil war hero Colonel Sonam Wangchuk, a Maha Vir Chakra recipient, has passed away. Celebrated as a proud son of Ladakh, his gallantry during Operation Vijay exemplified resilience and service. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and the Army lauded his courageous leadership, stating his legacy will inspire future generations. His life embodied courage, service, and unity.

title: Federal court hears new case against Trump's latest global tariffs
description: The judges intensely questioned lawyers for both the plaintiffs and the government about what certain terms mean, including what precisely the term “balance-of-payments deficits” meant when it was used in the Trade Act of 1974 and what it means today

title: Army delayed strike during Op Sindoor at terror camps to respect namaz: General
description: Indian Army chief General Upendra Dwivedi revealed Operation Sindoor's strike was deliberately delayed last year. Commanders ensured the operation avoided disrupting 'namaz' prayers at a terrorist camp, choosing a time when no one was engaged in worship. This flexible timing demonstrated a unique consideration, prioritizing a specific prayer period before executing the mission.

title: Deleted from Bengal rolls & added to Bihar list: TMC worker
description: A Trinamool worker from West Bengal alleges his name was deleted from the state's electoral roll and inexplicably added to Bihar's voter list. Krishnendu Mukherjee, a booth-level agent, discovered this discrepancy when his name, present in the draft roll, was marked 'under adjudication' and later deleted. He suspects a political conspiracy due to his active TMC membership.

title: 10 killed as overcrowded boat capsizes in Yamuna
description: The boat was ferrying around 30 passengers between Vrindavan and nearby areas when it reportedly collided with an iron pontoon bridge built across the river

title: An alternative proposal on Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhisthan Bill
description: State Higher Education Councils should be represented on the three councils envisaged under the Bill

title: IPL 2026 | Struggling CSK takes on DC, looks to snap losing streak
description: Though Chennai Super Kings’ mascot is a lion, Ruturaj Gaikwad and his men might need to invoke a similar spirit to revive their faltering IPL-19 campaign

title: Navy pushes for desi systems for Russian-built MiG-29K fleet to boost aircraft carrier ops
description: The Indian Navy is boosting self-reliance by inviting domestic firms to develop and manufacture critical subsystems for its Russian-built MiG-29K fighter jets. This initiative aims to enhance aircraft availability and reduce foreign dependency.  Indian companies will now develop automated test systems for the MiG-29K/KUB, crucial for maintaining combat readiness and strengthening national security through controlled supply chains.

title: Why India must define its own green factory standards now
description: As plants adopt solar power and resource-efficient systems, the absence of a unified certification limits global recognition

title: 'Copycat manifesto', BJP never delivers on its promises: TMC
description: Bengal's ruling TMC has labelled the BJP's election manifesto a "copycat" document, questioning the saffron party's track record on fulfilling promises. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee highlighted unfulfilled pledges like Rs 15 lakh bank deposits and job creation, while a TMC spokesperson accused the BJP of adopting their successful welfare schemes.

title: Lebanon says first contact with Israel held ahead of U.S.-brokered talks
Fire at Sam Altman’s home after Molotov attack; OpenAI HQ threatened
description: A 20-year-old man was apprehended after allegedly hurling a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's San Francisco residence, igniting an exterior gate. Shortly after, he reportedly threatened to torch OpenAI's headquarters. Police swiftly responded to both incidents, detaining the suspect. OpenAI expressed gratitude for the rapid police response and support in ensuring employee safety.

title: Day after Vijay's unreleased film leaks online, TVK blames rivals
description: TVK party blames Union minister L Murugan and Tamil Nadu's deputy CM Udhayanidhi Stalin for the online leak of actor Vijay's unreleased film 'Jananayagan'. The production house has filed a complaint seeking forensic investigation and legal action against those responsible for the unauthorized circulation.

title: Fendi, Tiffany inspire luxe board game trend, Young Designers India reimagine chess
description: YDI reinterprets the country’s ancient game through sculptural forms, architectural references and experimental materials

title: Bolstering deterrence through submarine dominance
description: Given the changing nature of warfare, it is essential to strengthen deterrence capabilities across land, air, and sea

title: No notices for code violations, EC now goes for FIRs straight
description: The Election Commission has issued no model code of conduct notices during ongoing polls, opting instead for FIRs for serious violations. This shift, attributed to CEC Gyanesh Kumar, aims to streamline enforcement within tight campaign schedules and leverage existing legal frameworks for offenses like communal remarks and personal attacks.

title: Migratory birds pose huge risk to flights, says wildlife body, rejects greenfield international airport plan in Puri
description: Existing evidence indicates a high risk of bird strikes as the Chilika Lake, located close to the proposed airport site, hosts thousands of migratory birds every year, says the Wildlife Institute of India

title: Kin try to give Veerappan a Robin Hood aura, join fray as 'defenders of Tamil nationalism'
description: Veerappan's daughter Vidhyarani and widow Muthulakshmi are entering electoral politics. They aim to portray the late forest brigand as a protector of Tamil rights. Vidhyarani is contesting from Mettur and Muthulakshmi from Krishnagiri. Both candidates are backed by parties supporting Tamil nationalism. Their campaigns focus on their father's legacy and local issues.

title: Pep Guardiola demands that his Manchester City be perfect after inconsistent season
At ICA conference, CJI Surya Kant underscores arbitration’s role in global economy
description: Chief Justice Kant expressed that, “it may be time to rethink the very expression ‘Alternative Dispute Resolution’ since it is no longer an alternative to litigation”.

title: Bengal, with polarised minority vote, makes Hindutva & security good poll planks, feels BJP
description: BJP's Bengal manifesto champions Hindutva and national security, promising a crackdown on infiltrators and implementing Uniform Civil Code. It also targets women and youth with welfare schemes, aiming to consolidate Hindu votes against TMC's alleged appeasement. The party stresses border security and curbing corruption, seeking to replicate its Assam success.

title: Two Indian students in UK, one from Mumbai, drown in separate incidents
description: Tragedy strikes the University of Hertfordshire as two Indian students, Mahesh Ramesh Khandagle and Leela Sai Reddy Mallireddy, have died in separate drowning incidents in the UK. Khandagle, 23, from Mumbai, drowned in Wales while testing water depth. Mallireddy, 24, from Andhra Pradesh, was rescued from the River Ouse but later passed away.

title: RBI repo rate at 5.25%: Impact on real estate sector
description: The apex bank holds the repo rate, offering predictability for EMIs and measured confidence across property markets

title: Konthuruthy river restoration: Kochi Corporation hands aid to 80 families
description: The assistance, part of the rehabilitation package, was transferred directly to the bank accounts of beneficiary families who submitted documents in accordance with government norms

title: Man crushed under bus during repair
55 workers, including 20 women, arrested for Manesar violence over wage hike
Monsoon readiness: Tushar Giri Nath issues instructions for flood-prone areas in Bengaluru
description: Nath has ordered drone documentation of ongoing works near Yelahanka Lake and called for regular desilting and continuous monitoring of the rainwater channel beneath the Kogilu Junction flyover

title: Deceptive recruitment of Indians for Ukraine war is trafficking: Supreme Court
description: The Supreme Court is examining serious allegations of human trafficking. Relatives claim Indians were lured to Russia for jobs or studies but were then forced into military roles. Their passports were taken, and they faced threats. The court has asked the solicitor general to investigate these disturbing reports.

title: Students to get textbooks by April 20: Sood
14 lakh tons of silt cleared, half of desilting work complete: Parvesh
JNU considers 5% admission quota for employees’ children
JCB driver held after two bodies found on DDA land
Off the boil: examining Varun Chakaravarthy’s sudden downturn
description: Until recently, the mystery spinner was the world’s top-ranked T20I bowler. But since the Super Eights at the 2026 T20 World Cup, the 34-year-old’s game has unravelled. What has led to this surprising dip in form? And can he return to his unplayable best during the IPL?

title: Oppn meet to forge women's quota bill stand on April 15
description: Opposition parties will meet April 15 to strategize on the women's reservation law amendments. Congress slammed the government for delinking the quota from Census 2026 and delimitation, accusing it of political maneuvering. Concerns were raised about the constitutional process and potential sub-quotas for OBC women, with a call to follow the census-delimitation-quota sequence.

title: How Lamine Yamal became the face of post-Messi Barcelona
description: After powering the club to the domestic treble last season, the boy wonder has helped drive the Blaugrana to the top of LaLiga and into the Champions League’s last eight. His goal-scoring, playmaking and dribbling have fuelled his dizzying rise to superstardom. Having inherited Barcelona’s iconic No. 10 shirt, there is no doubt the 18-year-old is the club’s leading man

title: Without naming Israel, India regrets Lebanon civilian deaths
description: India expressed deep concern over significant civilian casualties in recent Israeli strikes on Lebanon, following a US-Iran ceasefire. As a UNIFIL contributor, India finds the events disturbing, emphasizing civilian protection and respect for international law. The ceasefire's fragility is highlighted by confusion over Lebanon's inclusion.

title: Prioritise foundational issues in higher education in manifesto, Prof. Balagurusamy urges CM Stalin
description: In an open letter to M.K. Stalin, Prof. Balagurusamy appealed to him to focus on filling up vacancies in institutions, develop infrastructure, and plug leadership gaps like absence of vice chancellors in State-run Universities

description: The Chancellor also said that the existing six hours of class hours in schools in the country should be better utilized to improve quality education

title: The Hindu Education Plus will host a two-day ‘Career Counselling Fair’ in Chennai on April 11 and 12
Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry step up again with major charity effort
description: Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025, after over 10 years of being with the brand exclusively. While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision and his wife, Ayesha Curry, has been a supporter through all of it.

description: IOR Ministers in Mauritius, for conference overshadowed this year by the US-Israel war with Iran and peace talks

description: Participants took an oath to cast their vote in the upcoming polls

description: Estimates of destroyed goods in the godown are being done

title: 149 leopards died since in 14 months in Madhya Pradesh; road accidents cause 31% deaths
Turning up: On high turnout in Kerala, Assam, Puducherry polls
description: Voter enthusiasm is a sign of a healthy democracy

title: J&K: Two grenades found in mystery box in Rajouri
description: Security forces discovered a suspected IED-type box with two grenades in Rajouri's Sunderbani belt. This incident followed the detention of a school van driver in Jammu, who had multiple Pakistani contacts on his phone. Investigations are ongoing into the driver's suspected terror links.

title: Pakistan national team selector Misbah-ul-Haq forced to defend himself for working with PSL franchise
IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi grabs Orange Cap from Yashasvi Jaiswal; Dhruv Jurel leads RR’s victory over RCB

Asian Wrestling Championships: Meenakshi Goyat bags silver; Sujeet, Abhimanyou, Sandeep enter gold medal bouts
IPL: Sooryavanshi blitz scripts history, powers Rajasthan Royals to...
description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi powered Rajasthan Royals to a record 97/1 Powerplay while chasing 202 against RCB, smashing 57 off 18 in the first six overs with support from Dhruv Jurel. Earlier, Rajat Patidar scored 63 to lift RCB to 201/8. RR’s aggressive start, after strong bowling led by Ravi Bishnoi, put them firmly in control.

title: The frog that breaks its own bones to grow claws
Meet Nathan Byrd: 18-year-old who braved fire, saved family, dog
description: An 18-year-old from North Carolina, Nathan Byrd, heroically intervened when a grease fire erupted in his neighbor's home. Vaulting a fence and entering the smoke-filled house with a fire extinguisher, he successfully doused the flames before firefighters arrived. His quick thinking prevented injuries and significant damage, showcasing extraordinary neighborly courage.

title: T Natarajan opens up on pain behind DC comeback ahead of IPL 2026 clash against CSK: ‘Injury always in my mind’

description: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and The New York Times’ popular NFL reporter and insider, Dianna Russini, have sparked outrage among millions after pictures of them sitting together at an upscale hotel were leaked to the media. Amid all the mess, Dianna Russini’s old remarks have raised eyebrows.

description: With the NFL Draft approaching, Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini find themselves dealing with off-field attention after photos sparked speculation. Their professional history adds context, but not clarity. As reactions continue to grow, the situation highlights how quickly narratives can form in today’s media cycle, even without firm evidence.

$2 tn wipeout to emergency meeting: Anthropic’s AI becomes Wall Street’s biggest headache
description: US Treasury Secretary and Federal Reserve Chair urgently met with top bank CEOs to discuss Anthropic's new AI model, Claude Mythos Preview. This powerful AI can find software vulnerabilities, raising cybersecurity concerns for the financial system. Anthropic launched Project Glasswing to proactively fix flaws before attackers exploit them.

title: Two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai’s ‘Code Red’, Satya Nadella orders AI overhaul
description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has initiated a 'Copilot code red' to enhance AI performance and user experience, aiming to boost investor confidence amidst intense competition.  The company is allocating significant cloud capacity to Copilot development, with analysts suggesting Azure can still exceed expectations due to growing demand and pricing.

title: Meet the 80-year-old idli amma who sold idlis for Rs 1 so no one sleeps hungry
description: In Coimbatore, K. Kamalathal, known as 'Idli Amma,' continues her decades-long mission of providing affordable ₹1 idlis. Her unwavering generosity, even during inflation and the lockdown, gained national attention, leading to a house-cum-workspace built by Anand Mahindra. Her story highlights the enduring power of simple kindness and a strong work ethic.

title: Megan Thee Stallion on Klay Thompson romance and letting go of her mother
description: The Dallas Mavericks’ star player, Klay Thompson, has proven his love and loyalty towards the popular Grammy winner, Megan Thee Stallion, for close to a year of their high profile romance. While Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson seem happy together, the popular artist had gone through a lot in her life, including witnessing her mother’s brutal death in 2019.
